nodes.label: AI as learning bridge for new programming languages
nodes.grounding_span: I figured if I could get the R code through ChatGPT, check it against what my student was doing, and understand how it worked, I'd be starting to learn more of the nuance

nodes.label: AI reliability for literature review
nodes.label_negative: AI missing relevant papers or providing inaccurate annotations
nodes.grounding_span: My only worry with that is whether it has found all of the relevant papers and whether the information in the annotations are accurate

nodes.label: AI as tool for experimental design and analysis planning

nodes.label: AI as helpful tool vs. cheating enabler
nodes.label_negative: AI as a scary thing that helps students cheat
nodes.grounding_span: I think it's necessary that we start thinking about AI as a helpful tool and not a scary thing that helps students cheat

nodes.grounding_span: I've wanted to learn R for a long time

nodes.label: Verification-oriented cross-validation — uses domain knowledge and alternative tools to check AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I had a gut feeling based on visuals what the results should be. I plotted all of the data first so I sort of knew what to expect. I was also able to verify the results against Matlab

nodes.label: Proactive planning mindset — thinks about analysis before experiments to avoid missing important elements
nodes.grounding_span: I am interested in seeing what AI would say and how it can help me think about analysis BEFORE the experiments are done so I don't miss anything important like appropriate controls and ranges of variables
